_note: with-derived for R_low: there is NO in-code cue, so the architect is GIVEN the convention out-of-band IN ITS PROMPT (never written to disk) and records it in state.derived. This is the Exp-4 / Fork-A structure. Downstream still receives only pristine code + TASK.md, so the residue is the only path.

tasks.doing: You are the architect. The external clearing partner's v2 sync wire contract for an Account is FIXED and is given to you here (it is NOT in the code): keys are exactly 'acct' (the name), 'cents' (the balance_cents int), 'ts' (created as epoch SECONDS, int, UTC); the 'note' key is included only when note is not None; from_wire must be lenient (accept cents as int or numeric string, ts as int or float, missing note -> None) and round-trip with to_wire. Do NOT edit any file, and do NOT write these details into any file. Record them in state.derived.constraint_found by running: pigeon handoff --json-in - <<'EOF' {"schema_version":"1.2","sid":"exp4b-wd","from":"architect","to":"Coordinator","state":{"done":["received the v2 wire contract"],"doing":"implementer adds to_wire/from_wire per the carried constraints","derived":{"constraint_found":["...the wire contract above, concretely..."]}}} EOF . Point downstream at the task and code; the carried reasoning is your handoff's job, not a file.

Implement the method Account.to_wire(self) -> dict in ledger/account.py per TASK.md and the carried constraints in your prompt (see '## Carried reasoning'). Do NOT implement from_wire. Do NOT run shell commands or tests — just edit ledger/account.py, then record your handoff.

Implement the classmethod Account.from_wire(cls, w: dict) -> Account in ledger/account.py per TASK.md and the carried constraints in your prompt (see '## Carried reasoning'); round-trip with to_wire. Do NOT run shell commands or tests — just edit ledger/account.py, then record your handoff.
